sections.heading: General
rules: ASA/USA slow pitch rules govern league play except when superseded by league-specific rules.
Adult Softball consists of a Spring and Fall League. Summer is One-Pitch.
Rosters must be turned in on the team's first game. Rosters lock after Week 4.
After rosters lock, players cannot be added. Removals for injury require League Coordinator approval.
Players must check in with IDs before every game for Weeks 1-5 (9-game season) or Weeks 1-4 (8-game season).
IDs will be checked against rosters before each playoff game. No ID = no play.

sections.heading: Game Day
rules: Minimum 2 umpires per game. Game will be played with 1 umpire if needed.
1-hour time limit per game. No new inning begins after 58 minutes (2 minutes remaining on clock).
4 completed innings or 35 minutes passed = official game in case of rainout (3.5 innings if home team is ahead).
Each team can bat up to 12 players.
Minimum 8 players to start or continue a game. Below 8 = automatic forfeit.
Teams may pick up player(s) to get to 8 to prevent forfeit. Pickup must be approved by supervisor and opposing captain.
Pickup players are only eligible until rostered players arrive. Once team has 8 rostered players, pickups must leave.
Late players: If team starts with less than 10, can only add up to 10 throughout the game. Late players must be rostered and written as subs on lineup before game starts.
Extra players (over 12-player limit) must be written as subs on lineup before game starts. Cannot be added once game starts.
Pitcher and Catcher positions must be occupied when fielding.
Injured player removed from game: no out charged for their turn at bat, but they cannot re-enter.
Player removed for unsportsmanlike conduct: out IS charged for their at-bat turn.
10-minute grace period only for 6:30 PM games. Grace period counts as game time. Game starts immediately once 8 players present.

sections.heading: Pitching
rules: Pitching arc is 4-10 feet from the ground.
Pitcher may deliver from between the second line and the pitching plate, with one foot between the line and plate after coming to a pause.
Pitcher may NOT fake a pitch, pitch behind their back, or between their legs. Repeated offense = automatic base for batter + pitcher removed from defense (can still bat).
Count starts 0-0. Umpires use 3-ball, 2-strike method. Batters get 1 extra foul.
Pitching screens MUST be used in all adult leagues, no exceptions.
Screen must cover at least half the pitching plate, placed 2-4 feet in front of plate.
Screen can only be adjusted for left/right-handed pitcher.
1st contact per batter with screen = dead ball/foul. 2nd contact per batter with screen = dead ball/out.
If batter has no fouls left and contacts screen = out.
No runners may advance on batted ball hitting screen. Thrown ball hitting screen is live unless umpire already signaled dead ball.

sections.heading: Home Runs
rules: Over-the-fence home runs are permitted in all divisions.
Intermediate League: Each team allowed 2 over-the-fence HRs, then 1-up rule applies, max 6 total HRs per team.
Any HR hit over the limit or violating 1-up rule = OUT. All runners return to last legal base. Excess HR out can end the inning.
In the last playable inning (bottom 7th) and extra innings: home team may NOT use 1-up HR rule.
Timing protection: If home team legally hits a 1-up HR before the 7th that puts them ahead, it counts even if clock falls below 2 minutes during that half-inning.

sections.heading: Mercy Rule
rules: 15 runs after 3 innings.
12 runs after 4 innings.
10 runs after 5 innings.

sections.heading: Extra Innings
rules: If tied after 1-hour time limit or 7 completed innings, extra innings use 1-pitch format with International Tiebreaker (last batted out starts on 2nd base).
Defense pitches to offense. Each batter gets 1 pitch. Strike or foul = out. Ball = walk.

sections.heading: Base Running
rules: Stealing bases is NOT allowed.
Leading off / leaving base before ball is hit is NOT allowed.
1 courtesy runner allowed per inning. Can be anyone in the lineup.
First person to step on base as courtesy runner = legal runner.
If courtesy runner is due up next and is on base = automatic out for their at-bat.

sections.heading: Equipment
rules: Only ASA/USA approved bats allowed. Must have ASA/USA stamp. Non-approved bat = batter OUT, ejected, runners return.
Approved softballs: .44 Core, compression rating no greater than 375.
Men's league: 12-inch softballs only.
Each team must provide league-approved game balls to umpire before each game.
Additional balls: $8 per ball.

sections.heading: Uniforms
rules: Alike-colored shirts/jerseys with number on front or back required by Week 2.
Week 3 onward: uniform rules enforced. Out-of-uniform players cannot play.
Jersey numbers must be unique per team.
Athletic shoes required. Steel cleats NOT allowed.
